title: Missile Lock and Launch
summary: Proper procedure for acquiring lock and launching guided ordnance.
details: Guided missiles require target lock before launch. Maintain lock during acquisition phase - breaking lock wastes the cycle time. Launch when lock is solid and target is within missile effective range. Account for missile travel time against evasive targets. Fire-and-forget missiles allow immediate retargeting; guided missiles may require continuous tracking. Conserve missiles for high-value targets.
annotations: Missiles are limited and expensive - make them count.
Some targets have countermeasures that defeat missiles.
